title: Neocodon patulus
intro: Neocodon patulus, also known as the Chinese bugbane, is a perennial plant species native to China, characterized by its tall, upright stems and white or purple flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties, which may aid in treating various conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Neocodon patulus include iridoid glycosides, terpenoids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, Neocodon patulus is often used to prepare decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and sore throat.
taxonomy: Neocodon patulus is a species of flowering plant in the family Campanulaceae. It belongs to the genus Neocodon. The classification of Neocodon patulus places it within the family Campanulaceae, which is a family of flowering plants commonly known as the bellflower family. The Campanulaceae family is part of the order Asterales, which includes many other flowering plant species. Neocodon patulus is further classified as a member of the family Campanulaceae within the kingdom Plantae.
